patches.find: Independently, an 8/3 gear ratio in a hypocycloidal Spirograph produces an 8-cusped epicycloid, not a 2-lobed figure-8 — the named mechanism cannot produce the observed analemma shape.
patches.replace: Independently, an 8/3 gear ratio in a hypocycloidal Spirograph produces an 8-cusped epicycloid, not a 2-lobed figure-8 — the named mechanism cannot produce the observed analemma shape (formal hypocycloid/epicycloid geometry recorded in monitor/analyst/globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json, spirograph_analysis section, including the 8/3-gear → 8-cusped-curve mapping and the 2:1-gear reference that would actually produce a 2-lobed figure-8). The data_source="Spirograph" label and the absence of an analemma fetch in monitor.py were verified against github.com/john09289/predictions/blob/main/inject_ai_layer.py (V51.x repository snapshot, accessed 2026-04).
patches.rationale: ISS-1192 + ISS-1193: adds cross-reference to globe-fingerprints/WIN-019.json spirograph_analysis and github link for independent verification of Spirograph data_source label.

patches.find: with δ = 6,371 km — notably, this is Earth's mean radius, a globe-derived constant with no derivation from dome geometry.
patches.replace: with δ = 6,371 km — notably, this is Earth's mean radius, a globe-derived constant with no derivation from dome geometry. (We have been unable to locate the explicit Sub-H(r) formula in current V51.2 raw-text snapshots [01-home through 08-live-power]; the formula is reconstructed from the PRED-TOROID-002 registration metadata and the closed-toroidal architecture described on the model page.)
patches.rationale: ISS-1246: honest reconstructive provenance note — Sub-H(r) formula not located in V51.2 raw-text; attributed to PRED-TOROID-002 metadata and model page.

patches.find: and the toroidal geometry does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks.
patches.replace: and the toroidal geometry does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks — and a two-peaked surface fit is what any closed two-aperture topology would produce, so even this minimal predictive content is not uniquely diagnostic of the dome's specific toroidal architecture.
patches.rationale: ISS-1446: closes the concession-then-rebuttal gap — 'two peaks' is not uniquely diagnostic of dome's specific toroidal architecture.

patches.find: with none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry (the "observed values relabeled" failure mode documented in §6.1)
patches.replace: with A and C as curve-fit coefficients to observed pole-region magnitudes (neither derived from dome geometry — the "observed values relabeled" failure mode documented in §6.1) and λ a dome-internal length scale carried over verbatim from H(r) without any independent dome principle that would fix its value at 8,619 km
patches.rationale: ISS-1447: disaggregates A/C (curve-fit to observations) from λ (dome-internal length scale with no independent fixing principle). Preserves §6.1 link in A/C parenthetical.

patches.find: Shepherd et al., 2012, Science — ice-sheet mass loss; GIA adjustment modeled from LGM deglaciation loading history
patches.replace: Shepherd et al., 2012, Science for ice-sheet mass loss, with the GIA model itself from Whitehouse et al., 2012, GJI (W12a Antarctic GIA model from LGM deglaciation loading history)
patches.rationale: ISS-1384: correctly separates Shepherd 2012 (ice-sheet mass loss) from Whitehouse 2012 GJI (W12a Antarctic GIA model). Precision fix.

patches.find: Either way, the formula that derives from dome geometry fails by 170–1,430× depending on whether the comparison is apples-to-apples (absolute m/s²) or dimensionless-ratio (fractional variation).
patches.replace: The formula that derives from dome geometry fails by at least 170× — the dimensionless-fractional lower bound (90% predicted variation vs 0.53% observed) computed on different baselines and deliberately understating the discrepancy — and by 1,430× under the apples-to-apples absolute-m/s² comparison at Antarctic latitude.
patches.rationale: ISS-1396: replaces 'Either way... depending on' with 'at least 170× AND 1,430×' — both numbers simultaneously valid, not alternatives.

patches.find: Standard MHD simulations reproduce SAA splitting from core dynamics. The dome has no fluid-dynamics mechanism. The data was publicly available before the prediction.
patches.replace: Standard MHD simulations reproduce SAA splitting from core dynamics. The dome has no fluid-dynamics mechanism. The data was publicly available before the prediction (Part 3.1b post-hoc test; timestamp-anchoring error).
patches.rationale: ISS-1400: adds nav anchors to tldr_verdict so readers can verify post-hoc test and timestamp analysis.

patches.find: The Chapman mechanism (documented since Chapman (1919) and confirmed across 39 eclipses by Kim & Chang 2018) predicts the ensemble signal
patches.replace: The Chapman–Sq ionospheric framework (documented by Chapman 1919 and confirmed across 39 eclipses by Kim & Chang 2018) predicts the ensemble signal
patches.rationale: ISS-1512: grammatical disambiguation ('documented by' not 'since') and mechanism-naming precision (Chapman–Sq ionospheric framework).

patches.find: monitor.py Domain #7; PRED-TIER-3) as a structural coupling
patches.replace: monitor.py Domain #5; PRED-TIER-3) as a structural coupling
patches.rationale: ISS-1963: Domain #7 is GPS Sagnac Verification; κ lives under Domain #5 (EM-Gravity Coupling) per live-power.html.

patches.find: monitor.py Domain #7; PRED-TIER-3) as a structural coupling
patches.replace: monitor.py Domain #5; PRED-TIER-3) as a structural coupling
patches.rationale: ISS-1963: same Domain #7→#5 fix in WIN-014 detail_evidence.
ISS-1963: same Domain #7→#5 fix in SEC-6.14 prose (part6 section).

patches.find: at roughly 1,000× the precision of the Mohe spring gravimeter
patches.replace: at roughly 50–500× the precision of the Mohe spring gravimeter
patches.rationale: ISS-1967: replaces overstated 1,000× with defensible 50–500× range (spring gravimeter noise ~5–10 µGal; SG noise ~0.01–0.1 µGal → 50–1000× range; conservative 50–500×).
